summary: Independent historical/cultural/language review of writer commit e800a2c5 and the client_engineer REVIEW handoff accepts the task as content-lock preparation evidence only. The Beta audio/art/localization coverage manifest keeps notContentLockAcceptance true, preserves M6_GATE PASS_WITH_LIMITS and DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE, routes culture-specific visual/language/music/naming/symbol decisions to historical_researcher and Human Gate where required, and does not decide formal content lock, production culture assets, release/commercial scope, telemetry, accounts, cloud, server, multiplayer, or new dependencies.

tests_run.result: Worktree contains existing staged/modified lead/router/task state and writer handoff/route files plus untracked docs/M7_UI_Localization_Art_Handoff_Prompts.md; reviewer did not revert or overwrite them.

Commit changes are limited to Storybook, apps/web shell tests/surface routing, content-source coverage manifest, client-core read-model projection/fixture, UI coverage panel, writer handoff, and focused tests.

Coverage manifest has notContentLockAcceptance true, manualNodeBattleDecision DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE, all static boundary booleans true, static://m7 asset refs only, no http/cdn/cloud/multiplayer/telemetry-enabled markers, and all source/claim ids resolve through the M7 baseline or accepted M7 beta scenario source records.

tests_run.command: git diff e800a2c5^ e800a2c5 -- forbidden package/dependency paths
tests_run.result: No changes under sim-core, sim-ai, protocol, save-format, map-renderer, tools, CI, docs gates/roadmap/matrix, package.json, or pnpm-lock.yaml.

tests_run.command: git grep targeted boundary scans at e800a2c5
tests_run.result: No changed production path imports sim-core, fetches remote assets, adds telemetry/cloud/server/multiplayer behavior, or stores WorldState; WorldState hits are negative test assertions only.

decisions: ACCEPT is limited to historical/cultural/language reviewer acceptance of the writer evidence for this task; it is not formal M7 content lock acceptance.
The coverage manifest is correctly framed as m7-beta-content-lock-prep with notContentLockAcceptance true and risk.formal-content-lock-not-accepted routed to lead_orchestrator/Human Gate.

Culture-specific music, instruments, clothing, architecture, weapons, flags, scripts, religious imagery, names, titles, transliterations, and language quality remain unresolved review material; writer did not convert them into accepted production choices.

Neutral static://m7 audio/art slots are acceptable as Beta coverage evidence because their notes explicitly disclaim period instrument, melody, ritual, sacred sound, flag, script, temple, manuscript, court-symbol, and place claims.

The localization matrix exposes en-US and zh-Hans coverage plus stable key patterns, matched key counts, source/claim-linked guidance records, and review states; zh-Hans quality and sensitive names/titles remain LANGUAGE_REVIEW_REQUIRED.

The UI/client read-model changes are explanatory surfaces only; they do not write simulation state, introduce WorldState into store, or touch forbidden simulation/protocol/save/map-renderer boundaries.
Manual node battle remains DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE; this task does not reverse or reopen it.
risks: Formal content lock remains a future Human Gate and must not infer acceptance from this ACCEPT handoff.

The coverage manifest uses source.review.m7.baseline by reference through the accepted M7 beta scenario source records rather than duplicating that source record locally; this is traceable today but should be watched if manifests are later split or validated standalone.
Static neutral slots are coverage evidence, not finished period audio/art assets. Any later culture-specific production asset still needs source-specific historical/cultural review.
Language quality, sensitive names, titles, transliterations, and public-facing terminology remain review boundaries before formal lock.
requested_action: Lead may proceed with integration/reconciliation for this task while preserving that formal M7 content lock acceptance remains a future Human Gate and manual node battle remains DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE.
